cards.front: A set with a binary relation R, where either R(a,b) or not R(a,b), is called a zero-one matrix
cards.back: A matrix where each entry is either 0 or 1, representing the presence or absence of a relation between two elements

cards.front: A directed graph where there is a path from a node to another node if the relation is defined
cards.back: A graph where edges can point in different directions, representing the direction of the relation

cards.front: A Hasse diagram represents a partial order, showing the 'less than' or 'greater than' relationships between elements
cards.back: A diagram that uses a special type of drawing to represent a partial order, with nodes connected by edges if one element is less than or greater than the other

cards.front: An equivalence relation is a relation that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive, and is represented by a partition of the set
cards.back: A relation that satisfies three properties: every element is related to itself (reflexive), if a is related to b then b is related to a (symmetric), and if a is related to b and b is related to c then a is related to c (transitive)

cards.front: A partition of a set is a collection of subsets that are disjoint and whose union is the entire set, representing an equivalence relation
cards.back: A division of a set into non-overlapping subsets, where each subset contains elements that are related to each other
